affected_groups: Civic association-run schools and their local communities
Low-capacity, poorest municipalities that may lack administrative resources to run association contracts
Teachers employed under association-run or church-run maintainer structures
content.decision_question: Institutional pluralism and distributed responsibility versus centralized uniform state control of education
content.evidence_boundary: Contested and partly unknown: Polish association-model evidence is cited as a positive precedent, but whether similar outcomes (sustained schools without wage cuts or capacity gaps) would replicate in the Hungarian context, especially in the poorest villages, remains untested; no outcome data yet exists for the specific S3 legal design
content.tension: Church, civic and association-run maintainer plurality is itself a public good that distributes responsibility for rural education across society and preserves institutional diversity, so legal clarity strengthening this parallel maintainer track should be welcomed, not threatened.
content.title: Decision tension A13: S3
value_poles: centralized uniform state control of education
